description: Clergy, spiritual leaders, and religious practitioners.
jobs: Pope
A triple-crowned authority speaking as the supreme voice of the faith from the holy seat.
Cardinal
A red-robed advisor casting a vote when a new Pope must be chosen.
Archbishop
A crozier-bearing overseer of several dioceses, ordaining the bishops beneath him.
Bishop
A mitre-wearing governor of a diocese, consecrating churches and ordaining new priests.
Abbot
A rule-setting leader guiding a monastery's prayers, labor, and daily rhythms.
Abbess
A veil-draped mother superior governing a convent's devotion and discipline.
Prior
A second-in-command managing the daily rhythms of monastic life beneath the abbot.
Prioress
A capable administrator managing the convent's affairs when the abbess is occupied.
Archdeacon
A record-keeping official handling the bishop's administrative burdens and inspections.
Dean
A cathedral-running clergyman overseeing its daily operations and resident clergy.
Canon
A liturgy-keeping chanter maintaining the cathedral's daily offices and sacred calendar.
Priest
A vestment-wearing shepherd leading rites of baptism, marriage, and burial for the parish.
Chaplain
A traveling cleric conducting prayers and rites for a castle, ship, or noble household.
Friar
A barefoot wanderer preaching from village to village, begging alms and tending the poor.
Monk
A cowled figure copying manuscripts, tending gardens, and praying behind monastery walls.
Nun
A cloistered devotee living under sacred vows of poverty, prayer, and communal service.
Novice
A young aspirant studying scripture and enduring trials before taking lifelong vows.
Acolyte
A candle-lighting youth carrying incense and assisting the priest at the altar.
Deacon
A scripture-reading assistant helping with baptisms and charitable works in the parish.
Sexton
A bell-ringing caretaker digging graves and keeping the church building in working order.
Vicar
A flock-tending stand-in serving a parish in place of an absent rector.
Curate
A sermon-assisting helper supporting the parish priest with visits and daily services.
Hermit
A gaunt recluse praying and fasting alone in a wilderness cell, seeking divine closeness.
Pilgrim
A staff-carrying walker of long roads to holy shrines, bearing little but faith.
Crusader
A cross-marked warrior taking up sword to fight holy wars in distant lands.
Templar
A white-mantled guardian of pilgrims, protecting dangerous roads with blade and sacred oath.
Hospitaller
A wound-binding keeper of roadside hospitals, sheltering sick and weary pilgrims.
Theologian
A doctrine-debating writer of treatises on the nature of the divine.
Missionary
A foreign-land venturer converting the unconverted through word, deed, and endurance.
Almoner
A bread-and-coin distributor giving to the poor on a lord's charitable behalf.
Druid
A grove-dwelling channeler speaking for the ancient trees, stones, and streams.
Shaman
A trance-entering spirit-talker communing with ancestors and the unseen world.
Cleric
A prayer-channeling warrior of divine power, healing, protecting, and smiting in turn.
Exorcist
A rite-reciting specialist driving possessing spirits from afflicted, writhing bodies.
War Priest
A blood-spattered holy warrior blessing troops before battle and calling divine wrath amid the fray.
Faith Healer
A trembling-handed miracle seeker laying palms on the sick and praying for restoration.
Anchorite
A wall-sealed devotee living in a tiny cell beside the church, given entirely to prayer.
Stylite
A sun-blistered ascetic sitting atop a tall pillar for years, preaching to crowds below.
Flagellant
A self-whipping penitent marching in public processions, seeking penance through pain.
Penitent
A fasting supplicant performing acts of prayer and suffering to atone for grievous sins.
Confessor
A screen-sitting listener hearing whispered sins and granting absolution to the contrite.
Spiritual Director
A patient guide shepherding a soul's long journey through prayer, doubt, and devotion.
Spiritual Advisor
A wise counselor of nobles and commoners alike on matters of faith and conscience.
